title: stachys iva var. macedonica
intro: Stachys iva var. macedonica, also known as Macedonian hedgehog or Macedonian wood-woundwort, is a plant species in the mint family that is native to the Balkan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of fever and inflammation. Its leaves and roots have been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing stress, improving respiratory function, and aiding in the treatment of colds and flu. In herbal medicine, Stachys iva var. macedonica is often prepared as a tincture or a decoction, and its extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
